---
title: "The Method Section"      # <- your blog name. Swap for whatever you choose.
subtitle: "Methods and applications in economics and computer science: how methods work, when to use them, and real questions worked through with data. New posts on Fridays."
listing:
  id: recent-posts       # named so it can be placed under the routing below
  contents: posts
  sort: "date desc"
  type: default
  categories: true       # renders the clickable tag/category filter
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: true        # a search box over the archive
  feed: true             # generates index.xml (your RSS feed)
  fields: [date, title, reading-time, description, categories]
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: false
---

## Start here

Every post here is one of two kinds: a **method** --- how a technique works and
when to reach for it --- or an **applied** question worked through with data.
Two fields, one standard: show the work.

- **Economics & econometrics** --- filter with the **Economics** chip below.
- **Computer science & methods** --- filter with the **Computer Science** chip below.
- **Evaluating my work?** --- my [résumé (PDF)](resume.pdf) has the formal
  background; the posts themselves are the better sample of how I think.

The **category chips** below (Economics · Computer Science · Method · Applied)
filter the archive along either axis --- topic or type of post. They appear as
soon as posts are published.

*Following along:* new posts land on Fridays. Subscribe with any RSS reader via
the [feed](index.xml), or plug that feed into a service like Blogtrottr for
email --- no account with me required.

## Recent posts

::: {#recent-posts}
:::
